rules_fired.because: Hygroscopic and deliquescent solids absorb atmospheric water during each weighing event. Mass gain directly dilutes the effective molarity of the solution you prepare. Deliquescent solids (e.g., NaOH pellets) can become partially-dissolved slush within minutes in humid air, further confounding accurate weighing. Desiccator storage and fast weighing are the only controls for this.
rules_fired.cite: Sigma-Aldrich Handling Hygroscopic Compounds Technical Note. ASHP Guidelines on Handling Hazardous Drugs (weighing-by-difference rationale).

rules_fired.because: The SDS (Section 7 or 8) specifies ventilation controls or fume hood use for this reagent. This category spans volatile toxic solvents (pyridine, DMF, heptane), cytotoxic and genotoxic compounds (docetaxel, streptozocin, azoxymethane), and reagents whose vapours or aerosols pose an inhalation hazard. A chemical fume hood provides the primary engineering control — never handle these reagents on the open bench.
rules_fired.cite: OSHA Laboratory Standard 29 CFR 1910.1450. Manufacturer SDS Section 7/8.

rules_fired.because: Air-sensitive reagents deteriorate on contact with atmospheric oxygen or moisture. Linoleic acid oxidises to cytotoxic lipid peroxides within hours in open air. Rifampicin degrades to the inactive 3-formyl-rifamycin SV quinone on oxidation. Histamine autooxidises at alkaline pH and is also hygroscopic. DMF absorbs moisture and slowly hydrolyses to dimethylamine (an irritant). Keep bottles tightly capped between uses; aliquot into single-use volumes for high-frequency access. For oxygen-critical applications, consider dispensing under argon.
rules_fired.cite: Sigma-Aldrich product information: linoleic acid L1376, rifampicin R3501, DMF D4551. SDS Section 7.
